summary: W1625 is the latest source checkpoint and moves the direct-localization boundary to the underlying W987 branch-specific full-data provider. Follow-up source inspection found no nullary W987, W730, W986, or W1450 field-data constructor. Existing guard routes prove that unpacking W987 to W730/W986, W986 to W730/W984/W950, W730 to W716/W717, W1450 to W716/W717/W984/W950, and W990 primitive-unit components all expand the current one-input W987 frontier. This is a source-patch-needed handoff for the original four-task contract, not product success.
obligations_discharged: No new product-facing Lean source obligation is discharged by this handoff checkpoint.
The source inspection eliminates wrapper-only W987 follow-ups as useful next work because the current source already proves those routes expand or cycle around the one-input W987 frontier.
obligations_derived_or_eliminated: A W1626-style route from W987 back to W730/W986 is rejected because W1298 proves that unpacking expands the current W987 surface from one input to two.
A W1626-style route from W987 to W730/W984/W950 is rejected because W1299 proves that unpacking W986 expands the current W987 surface to three inputs.
A W1626-style route through W1450 is rejected as source progress because W1450 proves its W716/W717/W984/W950 construction surface expands the current one-input W987 provider.
known_gaps: No declaration currently defines OriginalFourTaskProductSuccess or originalFourTaskProductSuccess.
